title: 2D struct array parameter not treated as constant in generate-if
body:
This is a minimal example that shows the issue. Verilator reports an error when using a 2D array of packed structs as a parameter in a generate-if condition. The same setup works fine when using a 1D array.

```systemverilog
module Test;

  typedef struct packed {
    logic enable_feature;
  } unit_config_t;

  // Static configuration for 4 top units, 2 sub units each
  parameter unit_config_t UnitConfig[4][2] = '{
    '{ '{ enable_feature: 1'b1 }, '{ enable_feature: 1'b0 } },
    '{ '{ enable_feature: 1'b0 }, '{ enable_feature: 1'b1 } },
    '{ '{ enable_feature: 1'b1 }, '{ enable_feature: 1'b1 } },
    '{ '{ enable_feature: 1'b0 }, '{ enable_feature: 1'b0 } }
  };

  for (genvar i = 0; i < 4; i++) begin : top_unit
    for (genvar j = 0; j < 2; j++) begin : sub_unit
      if (UnitConfig[i][j].enable_feature) begin
        // Instantiate feature logic
      end else begin
        // Tie off outputs or insert stubs
      end
    end
  end

endmodule
```

## Command line

```
verilator test.sv --cc --exe --binary --main
```

## Output

```
%Error: test.sv:17:11: Expecting expression to be constant, but variable isn't const: 'UnitConfig'
                     : ... note: In instance 'Test'
   17 |       if (UnitConfig[i][j].enable_feature) begin
      |           ^~~~~~~~~~
%Error: test.sv:17:24: Generate If condition must evaluate to constant
                     : ... note: In instance 'Test'
   17 |       if (UnitConfig[i][j].enable_feature) begin
      |                        ^
%Error: Exiting due to 2 error(s)
```

## Version

Verilator 5.034  
Build date: 2025-02-24  
Rev: v5.034

## Notes

This only happens with a 2D array of structs. A 1D array with the same struct works without error.

Happy to help debug this. Where should I start looking?
